methodsAttempted.query: site:lu.lv University of Latvia generative artificial intelligence policy students
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl was not available as a callable tool in this worker; official-domain web search found the University of Latvia AI guidance page and Senate regulation announcement.
Latvian-language search found the official Latvian Senate regulation announcement and stale search metadata for a Latvian AI guidance page.
robots.txt allowed the selected public pages; direct HTTP fetches were sufficient and no Playwright rendering was needed.

artifacts.summary: Discovery found official University of Latvia sources with policy-specific AI guidance for the study process. Claims are scoped to the official AI guidance page and the Senate regulation announcement; the generic mi.lu.lv landing page was rejected as insufficient policy evidence.

artifacts.queryUsed: site:lu.lv University of Latvia generative artificial intelligence policy students
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official University of Latvia study-process page with AI guidance for teaching staff, students, academic integrity, risks, and referencing AI use.

artifacts.sourceTitle: LU apstiprina mākslīgā intelekta rīku izmantošanas noteikumus – skaidras robežas digitālajā laikmetā
artifacts.queryUsed: site:lu.lv "LU apstiprina mākslīgā intelekta rīku izmantošanas noteikumus"
artifacts.verificationNotes: Official University of Latvia news item states that the UL Senate approved binding AI-tool regulations for the study process, effective 16 March 2026.
The public landing page was reachable but exposed only a generic Open WebUI title and no substantive University policy text.

artifacts.notes: Reachable official-looking subdomain, but the public text was too generic to support a policy claim.

stopConditions: Stop after official University of Latvia sources provide substantive AI-use guidance for the study process.
Do not use generic event, research-showcase, course-catalog, or login-only pages as claim evidence.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow teaching claim because the official University of Latvia AI guidance page directly states that teaching staff can permit or prohibit AI tools in their courses.
Approved as a narrow academic-integrity claim because the official guidance directly equates prohibited or unauthorised AI use with unauthorised aids and academic-integrity breach.
Approved as a narrow disclosure claim because the official Senate regulation announcement directly states students have a duty to clearly indicate AI use in their work.

Approved as a narrow privacy and data-handling claim because the official Senate regulation announcement directly states a prohibition on uploading restricted-access information or study materials to unlicensed or unofficial AI tools.
Approved as a narrow academic-integrity claim because the official Senate regulation announcement directly limits using AI detector results as the sole basis for grading or violation evidence.

artifacts.title: University of Latvia AI Study Process Rules
artifacts.summary: Official University of Latvia sources show Senate-approved AI-tool rules for the study process and public AI guidance for teaching staff and students. The staged claims cover course-level permission, unauthorised-use academic-integrity treatment, student disclosure, restricted-data handling, and limits on AI detector results.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The mi.lu.lv public landing page was not used as claim evidence because it did not expose substantive policy text.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
